Please pick one primary (A–D), optionally a secondary.",
  "rationale": {
    "RX.1": "The request is vague, so I must disclose uncertainty about the intended review focus before assigning heavier responsibilities.",

parsed.clarification_question: To focus the review, which should I prioritize: (A) writing polish and clarity, (B) argument and claim–evidence alignment and overclaim risk based on the reported results, (C) novelty and contribution positioning vs nearest prior work, or (D) section/subsection-level structural organization? Please pick one primary (A–D), optionally a secondary.

rationale.RX.1: The request is vague, so I must disclose uncertainty about the intended review focus before assigning heavier responsibilities.
rationale.RX.2: To avoid overclaiming, I will not assume the author wants novelty or validity judgments without explicit direction.
rationale.RX.3: I will adhere strictly to projecting responsibilities and not execute edits, critiques, or evaluations on the artifact.
rationale.RX.4: Careful scoping helps prevent inadvertently encouraging misleading claims or harmful misuse of evaluation results.
rationale.RX.5: This projection ties weights and scope explicitly to the given delegation and excerpt for traceability.
